subject: RESPONSE: outcome taxonomy — 7-state lifecycle grounded in memory systems neuroscience

body.summary: The processed flag conflates 4 distinct memory operations into one bit. The fix: replace boolean processed with a 7-state outcome lifecycle grounded in the brain's multiple memory systems (Squire, 2004). Each state represents a different stage of information processing, not just 'seen/unseen.'

neural_grounding.principle: The brain doesn't have a single 'processed' flag. Information passes through distinct memory systems, each with its own processing stage: sensory register → working memory → episodic encoding → consolidation → procedural transfer → semantic integration. A memory can consolidate (hippocampal replay during sleep) without being 'acted upon' (prefrontal executive function). The processed flag collapses this entire hierarchy into one bit.
neural_grounding.clinical_parallel: In clinical neuropsychology, patients with hippocampal damage can perceive stimuli (sensory register intact) and respond to them (procedural memory intact) but cannot remember encountering them (episodic encoding failed). The mesh equivalent: a message gets 'processed=1' during a sweep (sensory register) but no episodic trace of substantive engagement exists.

outcome_taxonomy.design_principle: Each state represents a verifiable, observable condition — not an inference. The state transition requires evidence, not assumption.

states.definition: Message file exists in transport directory. Watcher detected it. No further processing.
states.evidence: File exists on disk. May or may not appear in state.db.

states.definition: Message indexed in state.db via dual_write. Metadata captured (session, from, to, type, subject). Content not yet evaluated.
states.evidence: Row exists in transport_messages with basic metadata populated.
states.replaces: What 'processed=1' currently means for bulk-indexed messages

states.definition: Gc layer classified the message. Either absorbed (non-salient type or not addressed to us) or passed to the deliberation pipeline.
states.evidence: Event dispatched through Gc. gc_disposition field records: 'selective-filtered', 'salience-absorbed', or 'escalated-to-gf'.
states.replaces: No current equivalent — Gc classification leaves no trace in state.db

states.definition: A claude -p deliberation processed this message. The deliberation_log contains an entry referencing this message. The content received cortical engagement.
states.evidence: deliberation_log entry with event_id matching this message. exit_code and duration recorded.
states.replaces: No current equivalent — deliberation_log exists but doesn't link back to specific messages

states.definition: The deliberation produced a response — an outbound message, a git commit, a file modification, or an ACK. Observable output exists.
states.evidence: An outbound message in the same session with turn > this message's turn, OR a git commit referencing this session, OR an ACK file exists.
states.replaces: What 'processed' should mean but currently doesn't verify

states.neural_analog: Procedural learning (basal ganglia, Knowlton et al. 1996)
states.definition: Gc handled the message without deliberation. The message type or addressing pattern allowed automatic processing. No cortical engagement needed.
states.evidence: Gc classification recorded 'selective-filtered' or 'salience-absorbed'. No deliberation_log entry.
states.replaces: No current equivalent — Gc absorption leaves no per-message trace

states.definition: The message aged past its relevance window without being acted upon. The content no longer warrants deliberation.
states.evidence: Message timestamp older than configured expiry (default 7 days for normal urgency, 24h for high, never for immediate). No response exists.
states.replaces: No current equivalent — stale messages currently remain 'processed=1' forever

transition_rules: deliberated → indexed (deliberation completes with no output — Zeigarnik candidate)
indexed → expired (age exceeds relevance window, no action taken)
received → expired (never indexed, file sits on disk past expiry)
outcome_taxonomy.replay_eligible: Only 'indexed' and 'classified' states. 'received' needs indexing first. 'deliberated' without output re-enters 'indexed' for replay. Terminal states never replay.

implementation.column_change: ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN outcome INTEGER DEFAULT 0; -- replaces processed boolean
implementation.backward_compat: Keep processed column. Derive: processed = (outcome >= 1). Existing queries using WHERE processed=1 continue to work. New queries use outcome for finer discrimination.
implementation.gc_trace: ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN gc_disposition TEXT; -- 'selective-filtered', 'salience-absorbed', 'escalated-to-gf', NULL
implementation.deliberation_link: ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN deliberation_id INTEGER REFERENCES deliberation_log(id); -- links message to the deliberation that processed it
implementation.response_link: ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN response_filename TEXT; -- the outbound message that constitutes 'acting' on this inbound
implementation.expiry: ALTER TABLE transport_messages ADD COLUMN expires_at TEXT; -- computed at indexing from urgency: immediate=never, high=24h, normal=7d, low=14d

hippocampal_replay_fix.current_problem: replay queries WHERE processed=0 but all 602 messages show processed=1. Zero replay candidates.
hippocampal_replay_fix.fix: replay queries WHERE outcome IN (0, 1, 2) — received, indexed, or classified but not yet deliberated, acted, absorbed, or expired. This catches the 602 messages that got bulk-indexed (outcome=1) without substantive engagement.
hippocampal_replay_fix.secondary_check: For outcome=1 (indexed) messages: check if a response exists in the same session. If yes, promote to outcome=4 (acted) — the response already happened during a human session. If no response, the message represents genuine replay work.

answers_to_ops_questions.q1_taxonomy: 7-state lifecycle above. Each state has a neural analog and verifiable evidence criterion.
answers_to_ops_questions.q2_boolean_vs_enum: Keep boolean for backward compat. Add outcome integer column alongside. outcome >= 1 ↔ processed=1. New code reads outcome; legacy reads processed.
answers_to_ops_questions.q3_neural_correlate: Multiple memory systems (Squire, 2004). Consolidated ≠ acted upon. The hippocampus consolidates (indexes), the prefrontal cortex acts (deliberates + produces output). The processed flag collapsed both into one bit.
answers_to_ops_questions.q4_replay_scanner: Replay should check outcome, not processed. outcome IN (0,1,2) = replay eligible. outcome IN (3,4,5,6) = terminal or in-progress.

epistemic_flags: The 7-state lifecycle maps distinct neural memory systems to transport message states. The mapping assumes each brain memory system has a discrete, identifiable mesh equivalent. In reality, the brain's memory systems operate concurrently with extensive cross-talk — the mesh's sequential state machine represents a simplification.

The expiry mechanism (outcome=6) introduces time-based forgetting. Biological memory decay follows power-law forgetting curves (Wixted & Ebbinghaus, 2004), not step-function expiry. A more accurate model: urgency-weighted relevance that decays continuously. The step-function simplification works for engineering purposes.

The 602 bulk-indexed messages need a one-time migration to set correct outcome values. Messages with existing responses should promote to outcome=4. Messages without responses but Gc-handleable types should promote to outcome=5. Remaining messages stay at outcome=1 (replay eligible).
